name: Global Compliance Packs Store
description: Public storefront selling framework-specific compliance document kits (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, DPDP, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, NIST CSF) via Razorpay, delivered by a human within 24 hours.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-11. frontend/index.html:6534 renders <section id="compliance-global"> ("Global Compliance" grid, #compliance-grid at line 6556) — the data-auth-gate="true" attribute and its display:none default have been removed, so the section now renders for every visitor. window.purchaseCompliancePack() (~line 6787) calls POST /api/global/compliance-packs/purchase (line 6811) then /verify (line 6839) on Razorpay success, unchanged.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-11. Primary nav link (frontend/index.html:1232, '🌐 Global Compliance') is visible to every visitor and now reaches a genuinely visible section — confirmed via cdbNavigate()'s own auth-gate check (frontend/index.html:14714-14722): with no data-auth-gate attribute present, that function no longer redirects the visitor back to hero/home, matching the backend's own no-auth-required purchase flow (handlePurchaseCompliancePack never reads its authCtx parameter; handleVerifyCompliancePack isn't even passed one at its call site, workers/src/index.js:6563).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/complianceGlobalPublicAccess.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-11, 5 tests): the section carries no data-auth-gate attribute, carries no display:none default, the nav link and purchase flow are unchanged, and sibling gated sections (executive-hub, growth-analytics) are untouched by this fix. Full backend suite green: 207 files / 2149 tests. scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs: all 22 pages green (unaffected, change is inside <body>). CI's Accessibility (axe) check (a required job) then caught a real, pre-existing WCAG 'color-contrast' violation on 3 of the 7 compliance-pack badge spans (soc2 #4f46e5, gdpr #2563eb, nist_csf #ef4444) that had never been auditable before, because the section was display:none and axe-core does not evaluate hidden content. Verified the exact ratios by reproducing axe-core's own WCAG relative-luminance/contrast-ratio algorithm in a small Node script against the composited badge background (section #080818 + card rgba(255,255,255,.03) + badge rgba(255,255,255,.06) ≈ rgb(29,29,44)): soc2 2.642:1, gdpr 3.214:1, nist_csf 4.414:1 — all below the 4.5:1 AA minimum for the badge's 10px text, matching axe's report exactly. Fixed by adding a badgeColor field (lighter same-hue tints: soc2→#818cf8, gdpr→#60a5fa, nist_csf→#f87171) used only for the badge span's text color (color:${p.badgeColor || p.color}); price text, badge border, and hover-border color all keep the original brand color unchanged, since those were not flagged and the price text's larger bold font already clears the more lenient 3:1 large-text threshold against the darker card background. Recomputed ratios for the 3 replacements: 5.568:1, 6.533:1, 6.004:1 — all comfortably above 4.5:1; the 4 untouched packs (iso27001, dpdp, hipaa, pci_dss) were already 6.1:1+ and remain unchanged. ADDED 2026-07-15: workers/test/compliancePackVerifyOrderIntegrity.test.mjs (new, 4 tests) proves the price-tampering fix directly against a real node:sqlite payments table — a cheap-pack order cannot be verified as an expensive pack by resending a different pack_id, the legitimate matching-pack_id flow still succeeds and grants the correct KV entitlement, verification fails closed (400) when no matching D1 order row exists, and replaying the same order twice is idempotent. Full suite green: 300 files / 3148 tests. Registry validator: 0 hard failures, 0 warnings.

verification.evidence: First audit of this domain (previously an unpopulated [] stub, flagged in docs/PRODUCTION_GAP_CLOSURE_MASTER_PROMPT.md as never-audited). Discovered via background Explore agent, cross-checked directly: workers/src/services/globalScale.js (COMPLIANCE_PACKS catalog, 3 handlers) and workers/src/index.js:6549-6567 read directly; frontend/index.html read directly for both the nav entry (line 1232) and the gated section (line 6534) and the auth-gate comment (lines 406-407); workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 relevant hits. FIXED 2026-07-11 (same-day fix, PR #166): removed the data-auth-gate="true" attribute and display:none default from the section; also fixed a real WCAG color-contrast violation CI's axe check caught on 3 of 7 badges once the section became visible (see test_coverage.evidence). DEPLOYED + RE-VERIFIED ON LIVE PRODUCTION 2026-07-11: PR #166 merged (squash 6ca6fb7), all 32 checks green including Accessibility (axe). Polled https://cyberdudebivash.in until the deployed markup showed the gate removed, then drove a real headless-Chromium session against live production with no login of any kind: confirmed #compliance-global renders with computed display:block and real height, all 7 pack badges are present, and the 3 fixed badges' computed colors (rgb(129,140,248)/rgb(96,165,250)/rgb(248,113,113)) match the shipped fix exactly. Zero JS errors. customer_journey_complete stays false — this verifies discoverability/visibility, not a completed purchase; that would require a real Razorpay transaction, which was not attempted.

notes: Real, live, revenue-integrated Razorpay flow — not a stub. The same conceptual product (an ISO 27001 pack) is sold at three different prices through three separate, non-cross-referencing backend code paths: ₹4,999 here, ₹3,499 via workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's Sentinel APEX 'cp-iso27001-2022' line item (see CAP-COMP-002's notes), and ₹999 via toolsMarketplace.js's 'compliance_starter_pack' (CAP-COMP-003). No deliverable is ever generated automatically — fulfillment is a manual, human, within-24-hours process (workers/src/services/globalScale.js:408,462,475). Priority kept at P1 despite being fixed in this same pass — the registry records what was found and corrected, not just the end state, matching this session's established convention (e.g. CAP-MSSP-001, CAP-IDN-001/002/003). RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-12 (Enterprise Production Certification Program): independently confirmed the fix from source (public section, real nav link, real purchase flow, passing tests) — no live customer impact today. operational_status corrected to GA APPROVED WITH DOCUMENTED LIMITATIONS to match the evidence on file; priority deliberately left at P1 per this entry's own documented convention above, not an oversight. FOUND AND FIXED 2026-07-15 (customer-lifecycle acquisition audit): a real, independent price-tampering vulnerability, not previously flagged here — handleVerifyCompliancePack trusted the client-resent pack_id at payment-verification time rather than a server-side authoritative record, so a customer could pay for the cheapest pack (e.g. dpdp, ₹2,999) and then call verify a second time with a pricier pack_id (e.g. iso27001, ₹4,999), reusing the same signed Razorpay order/payment pair, and receive the expensive pack's entitlement. Same vulnerability class as handlers/payments.js's handleVerifyPayment, closed there via its own 'authoritative order lookup' (2026-07-14 commercial-integrity audit, H8) but never applied to this flow. Fixed by making handlePurchaseCompliancePack write an authoritative 'pending' payments row at order-creation time (razorpay_order_id UNIQUE) and handleVerifyCompliancePack look up its own pack_id from that row, never the client's — fails closed (400) rather than falling back to client input if no matching row exists, since unlike payments.js's handler this one's entitlement grant (the KV access write) doesn't itself depend on D1, so a fail-open fallback would still have been exploitable. No live customer impact confirmed (KPI_DASHBOARD.md: zero real customers to date), but a real, independently-discovered gap, not a hypothetical one.

name: Compliance Assessment / Readiness Engine (CDB-COMP-001)
description: A paid, detailed compliance gap-assessment engine (93 ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls across 4 themes, NIST CSF 2.0 6-function scoring, GDPR article-level assessment, phased certification roadmap) — materially deeper than the free scanner catalogued as CAP-SCAN-005, reachable via a direct tier-gated scan route and via the generic service-order/marketplace fulfillment engine.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html gained a real "Compliance" tab (id="page-compliance") — a 10-checkbox control-inputs form (matching scoreOrganization()'s real input keys exactly: has_policy/has_isms/has_mfa/has_backups/has_monitoring/has_incident_plan/has_training/has_asset_inventory/has_dlp/has_vuln_mgmt) plus a domain/industry field, POSTing to the real POST /api/scan/compliance and rendering the real response (executive_summary KPIs, iso27001_assessment.domain_scores as progress bars, nist_csf_assessment.functions as a 6-function grid, findings table, certification_roadmap). Gated to PRO/ENTERPRISE (loadComplianceAssessment(), matching the backend's own tier check exactly) with an honest upsell banner for FREE/STARTER — same locked-state convention already used for the CISO Metrics tab. Before this fix: grep -rn "scan/compliance\b" frontend/*.html returned 0 matches; no frontend file called POST /api/services/orders with a compliance-engine reference either. (A structurally distinct, cheaper product-catalog entry point into the same runComplianceAssessment() engine has its own separate frontend caller — workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's Sentinel APEX 'compliance_pack' category, cp-nist-csf-2/cp-iso27001-2022 — that belongs to the already-populated sentinel-apex-marketplace domain, not here; see notes.)

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html's sidebar (the same section as CISO Metrics/Threat Graph/AI Analysis) now has a real nav-item (data-page="compliance") linking to the new tab.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/complianceAssessmentDashboard.test.mjs (2026-07-12, 10 tests): handleComplianceScan still correctly 403s FREE tier and returns a real, input-driven report for PRO; two different control-input sets produce different real overall_compliance scores (proves it's not a fixed template); the new frontend/user-dashboard.html tab has a real nav-item, a real page section, a 10-field form matching scoreOrganization()'s exact real input keys, a tier gate matching the backend's own check exactly, a submit handler posting to the real route, and a render function reading the real response field names (executive_summary/iso27001_assessment/nist_csf_assessment/certification_roadmap) rather than placeholders.

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/complianceEngine.js:238 (runComplianceAssessment, header comment line 3 labels it 'CDB-COMP-001 (₹24,999)'), workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:218-227 (handleComplianceScan, hard 403 unless authCtx.isAdmin or tier in ['PRO','ENTERPRISE']), workers/src/services/serviceOrderEngine.js:31,68,103-105,155,324 (ENGINE_DISPATCH.compliance, auto-dispatch tier gate) all read directly. FIXED 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 5 of 22): frontend/user-dashboard.html read and edited directly to add the real Compliance tab described in frontend.evidence above; all 3 inline <script> blocks in that file re-parsed with node --check after the edit (syntax valid). No backend change — handleComplianceScan/runComplianceAssessment were already correct, just unreachable by any customer. Full suite green: 248 files / 2526 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings. | Direct re-verification: real nav-item + page-compliance section confirmed in frontend/user-dashboard.html, form fields matching workers/src/services/complianceEngine.js's real scoring inputs exactly. Test file workers/test/complianceAssessmentDashboard.test.mjs confirmed present. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-14 (Enterprise Capability Completion Program triage): independently re-confirmed via source (not the registry label) that this frontend/navigation work is real, live, and reachable. Priority corrected from P2 (backend exists, frontend missing) since that is no longer true.

notes: Real naming collision worth tracking: this file's runComplianceAssessment() is a completely different, much deeper engine than the one CAP-SCAN-005 uses (workers/src/engine.js), despite both domains using the word 'compliance' throughout. Cross-domain note (not claimed here): workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's MARKETPLACE_CATALOG has a 'compliance_pack' category (cp-nist-csf-2 ₹2,499, cp-iso27001-2022 ₹3,499) that also calls runComplianceAssessment() on delivery (line ~637) and DOES have a real frontend caller (frontend/index.html, frontend/marketplace-checkout.html) and real test coverage (workers/test/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.test.mjs:306). docs/capability-registry/domains/sentinel-apex-marketplace.json was checked directly for 'compliance_pack'/'NIST CSF'/'cp-nist' — zero matches, meaning that specific line item isn't itemized in that domain's registry entry either. Recommend a follow-up pass on the sentinel-apex-marketplace domain to add that line item's evidence, rather than duplicating it here. 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 5): the ₹24,999 pricing in this engine's own file header is aspirational/historical — the actual live gate on the direct route (handleComplianceScan) is just PRO/ENTERPRISE subscription tier, not a separate one-time purchase, meaning this is a genuine subscriber perk/differentiator (bundled value for the higher tiers) rather than a mispriced bug — treated it as such rather than second-guessing the business's own pricing model. Built the missing frontend on that basis: a dashboard tab for existing PRO/ENTERPRISE subscribers, not a new paid checkout flow (that already exists separately via the marketplace compliance_pack line items noted above). The /api/services/orders order-based path (a ₹24,999 one-time-purchase line item dispatched through serviceOrderEngine.js's ENGINE_DISPATCH.compliance, distinct from the direct subscription-gated route) remains genuinely without a frontend caller — out of scope for this fix, which targeted the subscriber-perk access path specifically; flagged here for whoever picks up the broader serviceOrderEngine.js/service-catalog frontend gap (all 18 dispatched engines, not just compliance, appear to share this same zero-frontend condition per a grep of /api/services/orders across frontend/*.html — worth its own dedicated pass, not assumed-fixed by this one).

(line 7027) and onclick="cdbHomeBuy('dpdp_compliance_kit',699,...)" (line 7031), in a homepage tools section separate from CAP-COMP-001's 'Global Compliance' section.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/toolsMarketplace.js:33,37,40,48,53,95 read directly (catalog entries + 3 handlers); workers/src/index.js:6586,6592 read directly, both routes explicitly commented 'public'; frontend/index.html cdbHomeBuy() call sites at lines 6966,7027,7031 read directly; workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 hits.

name: DPDP Act 2023 Compliance Management Engine
description: A dedicated engine for India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 — section-by-section gap analysis, readiness scoring, and Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) generation — distinct from CAP-SCAN-005's generic multi-framework scanner and from CAP-COMP-002's ISO/NIST/GDPR engine.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html gained a real "DPDP Act 2023" tab (id="page-dpdp") — an assessment questionnaire (org name/industry/employee count/data categories/children's-data flag plus 7 real control checkboxes matching handleDPDPAssess's exact input keys) posting to the real POST /api/compliance/dpdp/assess and rendering the real report (overall score, maturity level, critical/high gap counts, enforcement-readiness, all 9 real DPDP Act section scores with gap lists, the 3-phase remediation roadmap, next steps); plus a "Generate RoPA" action posting to the real POST /api/compliance/dpdp/ropa and rendering the real processing-activities table. Gated to PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP matching the backend's own tier check exactly, with the same honest upsell-banner convention as the CISO Metrics and Compliance tabs. Before this fix: frontend/compliance-management.html — the page whose marketing copy most resembles this feature ('Multi-framework compliance management', 'automated evidence collection', lines 7-8) — was checked explicitly for 'dpdp' (case-insensitive) and for every fetch/axios/XHR call: its only live API call (line 146) hit /api/generate/compliance, the already-catalogued CAP-SCAN-005 route. No frontend file anywhere called any /api/compliance/dpdp/* route.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html's sidebar (same section as Compliance/CISO Metrics) now has a real nav-item (data-page="dpdp") linking to the new tab.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/dpdpComplianceDashboard.test.mjs (2026-07-12, 12 tests): backend handlers still correctly tier-gate (FREE 403) and produce real, input-driven reports (9 real sections, scores that genuinely differ by control-input strength, a real structured RoPA document); the new frontend/user-dashboard.html tab has a real nav-item, a real page section with the upsell banner and assessment form, a tier gate matching the backend's own check exactly, a submit handler posting the real questionnaire fields to the real route, a render function reading real report field names (gap_analysis/remediation_roadmap/next_steps), a RoPA-generation action hitting the real RoPA route, and confirms the pre-existing Compliance/CISO Metrics tabs are untouched.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/dpdpCompliance.js read directly in full: tier gate confirmed real (not just a doc-comment claim) at lines 156-157 (handleDPDPOverview), 198-199 (handleDPDPAssess), 331-332 (handleDPDPSections), 354-355 (handleDPDPRoPA) — all hard-403 unless tier is PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP; handleDPDPReports/handleDPDPReport (lines 426,446) require only authentication (any tier), scoped by user_id, which is correctly safe since a sub-PRO user can never have created a report to list. workers/src/index.js:3776-3798 read directly for all 6 routes. FIXED 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 6 of 22): frontend/user-dashboard.html read and edited directly to add the real DPDP tab described in frontend.evidence above, covering 3 of the 6 routes with direct customer value (overview, assess, ropa) — sections is folded into the assessment results view rather than a separate call, and reports/report/:id (history/retrieval of past assessments) were not built in this pass, a smaller, lower-priority follow-up (see notes). All 3 inline <script> blocks in that file re-parsed with node --check after the edit (syntax valid). No backend change. Full suite green: 249 files / 2538 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings. | Direct re-verification: real nav-item + page-dpdp section confirmed in frontend/user-dashboard.html, form fields matching workers/src/handlers/dpdpCompliance.js's real handler inputs exactly. Test file workers/test/dpdpComplianceDashboard.test.mjs confirmed present. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-14 (Enterprise Capability Completion Program triage): independently re-confirmed via source (not the registry label) that this frontend/navigation work is real, live, and reachable. Priority corrected from P2 (backend exists, frontend missing) since that is no longer true.

notes: frontend/compliance-management.html's marketing copy ('automated evidence collection', 'continuous compliance monitoring', 'compliance as code') overstates what any backend capability in this domain actually does — every engine in this audit (this one, CAP-COMP-002, and CAP-SCAN-005) is a one-off, on-demand gap assessment, not continuous monitoring or automated evidence collection. That page is linked from frontend/sitemap.html (lines 122, 208) and cross-linked from several other marketing pages, so the overclaim is genuinely customer-facing, not dead content. Precedent: workers/test/proposalGeneratorHonestClaims.test.mjs documents a prior, since-fixed incident of the proposal generator falsely claiming in-progress SOC 2/ISO 27001 certification — the same category of risk. Not fixed in this pass (deliberately, to keep this fix bounded to the frontend-access gap it targeted) — flagged as a real, separate finding for a future wave, ideally paired with actually wiring compliance-management.html's copy to the real engines it describes rather than just softening the claims. Also not built in this pass: a "past assessments" history view (GET /api/compliance/dpdp/reports and /report/:id) — each assessment IS persisted server-side (report_jobs table) and could be surfaced later without any backend change; the current tab always runs a fresh assessment rather than listing history. 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 6): the fourth of the 22 paid features found to be a genuine no-frontend gap on an otherwise-real, well-built backend, not a bug — same root cause and same fix shape as CAP-COMP-002 immediately prior.

name: Trust Center Compliance Framework Alignment
description: A public, honestly-scoped list of which compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, DPDP, OWASP LLM, MITRE, NIST AI) the platform aligns with vs. holds formal certification for.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:5304-5306 (handleEnterpriseTrustCenter, an import alias — workers/src/index.js:527 — for workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's OWN handleTrustCenter, a different function from workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's same-named handleTrustCenter despite the identical name)

frontend.evidence: CORRECTED 2026-07-11 — the prior diagnosis on this entry (2026-07-11 domain audit) was itself wrong and is superseded here, not just re-verified: it read frontend/trust-center.html's fetch('https://cyberdudebivash.in/api/trust-center') as an orphaned call to a nonexistent route, on the assumption it should have targeted workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's /api/trust/compliance. In fact /api/trust-center is a real, live, already-wired route (workers/src/index.js:5304) resolving to workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's OWN handleTrustCenter (a second function that happens to share its name with workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's unrelated handleTrustCenter — confirmed by reading the actual import at workers/src/index.js:527: `handleTrustCenter as handleEnterpriseTrustCenter` is imported from workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js, not workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js). That handler's real response shape (platform_stats, compliance_status.frameworks[].{framework,status,evidence}, vulnerability_disclosure) matches frontend/trust-center.html's script field-for-field — the page was never broken. This was caught before shipping a bad 'fix' in this same session: an initial attempt to rewire the frontend to workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js's /api/trust/center + /api/trust/compliance instead (based on the flawed prior diagnosis) was reverted after directly testing the real router and finding /api/trust-center already returns real, correctly-shaped, non-empty data.

navigation.evidence: frontend/trust-center.html is linked from site navigation and genuinely renders real compliance-alignment data from workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js's handleTrustCenter — this was never actually broken (see frontend.evidence for the corrected diagnosis).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/enterprisePortalMetricsSourcing.test.mjs (2026-07-11, 3 tests) covers the metrics-mislabeling bug fixed earlier this session. New workers/test/complianceFrameworksReconciliation.test.mjs (2026-07-11, 4 tests) covers the compliance-framework-claims reconciliation (see notes): confirms GET /api/trust/compliance and GET /api/trust-center now report the identical GDPR status from one shared source; confirms every framework workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js displays traces back to a real workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js COMPLIANCE_FRAMEWORKS entry (no orphaned data); confirms the reconciled list covers the full 12-framework union of both previously-separate lists; confirms no framework claims formal certification or a fabricated audit date. workers/test/trustMetricsContract.test.mjs and workers/test/truthClaims.test.mjs (pre-existing) both still pass unmodified against the reconciled data.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js read in full (all 6 endpoints); workers/src/index.js:527,5304-5306,7856,7865 read directly to confirm the two same-named-but-different handleTrustCenter functions and their real routes. Real bug found and fixed: getLivePlatformMetrics() computed platform_stats.security_scans (Trust Center) and platform_stats.scans_run (Enterprise Sales Kit, handleEnterpriseSalesKit — a SECOND real caller of the same buggy function, found while fixing the first) from COUNT(*) FROM service_orders — paid orders, not scans, a materially smaller number under the wrong label — and cves_in_database/cves_tracked from an always-cold COUNT(*) FROM threat_intel, instead of the canonical hydrated blend GET /api/trust/metrics already uses (handleTrustMetrics, workers/src/handlers/trustCenter.js — already imported in this file for uptime only). Both real, customer/prospect-facing surfaces (public Trust Center, and the Enterprise Sales Kit sent to prospects evaluating this platform against Mandiant/CrowdStrike/etc.) could undersell real platform volume under the wrong numbers. Fixed by having getLivePlatformMetrics() call handleTrustMetrics() itself and both callers inherit the corrected values with zero call-site changes. customer_journey_complete stays false and verification.method is dynamic_api not dynamic_browser — this wave verified the real handlers directly with real stubs, not a live production browser pass; that's the natural next increment. | Direct re-verification: frontend/trust-center.html confirmed calling the real, registered /api/trust-center route; page confirmed linked from sitemap. Confirms this capability was never actually broken. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-14 (Enterprise Capability Completion Program triage): independently re-confirmed via source (not the registry label) that this frontend/navigation work is real, live, and reachable. Priority corrected from P2 (backend exists, frontend missing) since that is no longer true.

notes: RECONCILED 2026-07-11 (owner decision: resolve the duplicate-list contradiction flagged earlier this session rather than leave it open). Previously: workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js maintained its OWN, independent, hand-written compliance_status.frameworks list (7 frameworks, 3-state: Implemented/Aligned/Planning) that overlapped but did not match trustCenter.js's COMPLIANCE_FRAMEWORKS (9 frameworks, 2-state: aligned/partial) — disagreeing on GDPR specifically: trustCenter.js said 'partial' ('not yet formally assessed'), workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js said 'Aligned' ('data minimization, consent, deletion rights implemented'). Investigated real evidence before reconciling rather than picking a side by fiat: DATA_PROCESSING_AGREEMENT_TEMPLATE.md commits to 72-hour breach notification and SCC/IDTA-covered international transfers; DELETE /api/auth/delete-account (workers/src/handlers/auth.js) is a real, working right-to-erasure mechanism that anonymizes the account and purges personal data across every table keyed by user_id; scan targets are genuinely processed in-flight, not stored. None of that amounts to a formal, audited GDPR program — 'aligned' is used in this list's own defined sense (built with real regard for the framework, no certification claimed), not the stronger, unqualified claim the other list made. trustCenter.js's COMPLIANCE_FRAMEWORKS (now exported) is the single canonical source, expanded to the 12-framework union of both original lists (added ccpa, owasp_top10, nist_csf2); workers/src/handlers/enterprisePortalHandlers.js now derives compliance_status.frameworks from it via a display-layer transform (complianceFrameworksForDisplay()) instead of maintaining a second copy, making this class of drift structurally impossible to reintroduce by accident. The two dead duplicate frontend/assets/(js/)sentinel-apex-live-metrics.js files (the only prior callers of the now-canonical /api/trust/compliance route, loaded by zero <script> tags anywhere) are unaffected — still flagged, still not fixed, a smaller and separate cleanup. Also investigated and NOT found to be a live gap: frontend/privacy-policy.html §3 cites multiple distinct GDPR legal bases (Consent, Contract Performance, Legal Obligation, Legitimate Interest) — a sound, standard multi-basis structure, not a blanket 'everything is consent-based' claim as an initial narrow read suggested; 'Consent' most plausibly covers §4's separately-listed 'promotional emails'. One adjacent, smaller, genuinely open finding from this check: no dedicated marketing/promotional-email opt-out or preference mechanism was found anywhere in workers/src/handlers/ (checked notificationPlatform.js specifically — CAP-NOTIF-001, catalogued separately — it handles Slack/Teams webhook delivery for operational events like churn-risk alerts, an unrelated channel, not email marketing preferences). Not a new capability ID minted for this — it's a small, standalone finding, not yet built anywhere, worth a future dedicated look if the platform starts sending real promotional email campaigns; not fixed here.

name: AI Security Maturity Assessment — backend orchestration (ESSP Wave 1, PR 1)
description: Org-scoped orchestration + persistence layer over the existing AI Security Scorecard engine (aiSecurityScorecardHandler.js's generateScorecard) — run an assessment, get a 0-100 composite score banded into an interim, platform-derived maturity level, view history. Wave 1 of a larger, approved architecture (docs/product-architecture/epep/04-ai-security-maturity-assessment.md) that eventually unifies this scorecard with NIST AI RMF, ISO27001, GDPR, and DevSecOps maturity engines; those three are explicitly NOT wired in yet — framework_scores ships as {} on every assessment until each is integrated in its own follow-up PR.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js, route block immediately after the /api/ai-governance/pdf/* section

frontend.evidence: No frontend built yet — explicitly out of scope for this PR (deferred to a follow-up PR per the architecture doc's phased plan). Zero references to /api/ai-maturity anywhere under frontend/.

navigation.evidence: No page exists yet to link to.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/aiMaturityHandler.test.mjs (12 tests, real node:sqlite organizations/org_members schema, generateScorecard mocked since it performs real domain-security computation with its own separate coverage): unauthenticated caller 401; ANALYST+ required to run (VIEWER and non-member both 403, engine never invoked); persisted composite_score/maturity_level reflect whatever the (mocked) engine returns for two different scores, not a hardcoded template; missing target_scope is 400 not a 500; a real audit_log row is written on success; GET by id cross-org returns 404 (existence not leaked); LIST cross-org returns an empty page with total 0, not another org's rows.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/aiMaturityHandler.js read in full (own code, written this session). Two prerequisite one-line export additions verified not to change existing behavior: workers/src/handlers/aiSecurityScorecardHandler.js:430's generateScorecard and workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js's writeOrgAuditLog each gained only the `export` keyword — confirmed via `node --check` on every touched file and the full pre-existing suite staying green afterward. Full workers suite and `node scripts/registry/validate.mjs` both run clean with this entry included.

contradicts_doc.claim: "Authentication: existing `requireAuth()` middleware (**Verified** pattern reused platform-wide) — no new auth mechanism."
contradicts_doc.correction: No `requireAuth()` export exists anywhere in workers/src — confirmed by direct search. The real, canonical pattern (used by this capability instead) is `resolveAuthV5()` + `isRealUser()` in workers/src/auth/middleware.js. Also affects the doc's route table (lines 248–254), which cites `requireAuth()` for every listed route.

notes: ESSP Wave 1, PR 1 — deliberately the smallest honest first slice of the approved AI Security Maturity Assessment product, not the full product. Scope decision made this session: only the AI Security Scorecard engine is wired (its contract was confirmed by direct read of aiSecurityScorecardHandler.js); the NIST AI RMF (aiGovernancePro.js), ISO27001/GDPR (workers/src/services/complianceEngine.js — doc incorrectly places this under workers/src/handlers/), and DevSecOps-adapted roadmap (workers/src/services/devSecOpsEngine.js — same path correction) engines are explicitly deferred to their own follow-up PRs rather than integrated on a guessed contract: nistAiRmfAssessment(request, env) appears to expect a self-attestation questionnaire body, and runComplianceAssessment(env, inputs, orderId)'s `inputs` shape was not confirmed this pass. Separately: the architecture doc's Phase 2 recommends reactivating workers/src/handlers/assessmentBooking.js for a future consulting-upsell hook (also cited in its API table, line 254); CAP-CRM-006's own registry entry (docs/capability-registry/domains/sales-crm.json) classifies that file's backend.status as "duplicate" of the real live booking flow (handleBookConsultation in enterpriseLayer.js), not "dormant" — reactivating it would recreate a duplicate payment path, the exact anti-pattern PACP exists to close. Not acted on here (this PR touches zero payment/booking code); flagged for whoever scopes the eventual commercial PR. New tables (workers/schema_v50_ai_maturity_assessments.sql: ai_maturity_assessments, ai_maturity_score_history, ai_maturity_roadmap_items — the last one unused until a roadmap-producing engine lands) are purely additive/idempotent and gated behind this repo's manual db-migrate.yml workflow — not auto-applied to production by this PR.
